_about: AI category (AIAgent, AiDeployment, AiEndpoint, AiJob, AiJobSkill, AiPlatform, AiPlatformDefinition, Copilot, LanguageServer). The Platform/Deployment/Endpoint stack chains together: platforms hold provider configuration, deployments link a model to a platform with cost settings, endpoints expose deployments with default options. Jobs run prompts through endpoints with allowed skills.

GET /api/v1/aiagent.description: Returns AI agents in the organisation, augmented with avatar image, total job costs in the deployment currency, count of active jobs, and count of pending non-dry-run results. Optional `metadataFilter` narrows by key/value metadata pairs. Requires the Management.AiAgents RACI claim.

parameters.metadataFilter: Optional key/value metadata filters as repeated `metadataFilter[key]=value` parameters.

GET /api/v1/aiagent/{id}.description: Returns a single AI agent with name, description, instructions, base64 avatar, and metadata. Requires the Management.AiAgents RACI claim.

POST /api/v1/aiagent.description: Creates an AI agent with the supplied name, description, instructions, and metadata. Records an audit-log change set and adds the calling user as the agent's owner. Requires the Management.AiAgents RACI claim.

PUT /api/v1/aiagent/{id}.description: Updates an AI agent's name, description, instructions, and metadata. Records an audit-log change set comparing the before and after state. Requires the Management.AiAgents RACI claim.

DELETE /api/v1/aiagent/{id}.description: Deletes an AI agent. Returns 400 with validation details when the agent is still linked to one or more AI jobs (delete the jobs first). Requires the Management.AiAgents RACI claim.

GET /api/v1/ai/admin/platform.description: Returns the AI platforms configured for the organisation, ordered by the backing data store. Platforms hold provider-level configuration used by deployments.

POST /api/v1/ai/admin/platform.description: Creates a non-system-managed AI platform from a platform definition. Persists provider display name, configuration values, and request limits.

GET /api/v1/ai/admin/platform/{id}.description: Returns one AI platform by id.

PUT /api/v1/ai/admin/platform/{id}.description: Updates a non-system-managed AI platform. The platform definition cannot be changed; secrets are injected from the existing platform where needed.

DELETE /api/v1/ai/admin/platform/{id}.description: Deletes a non-system-managed platform after verifying that no AI deployment references it. Returns 400 (validation) when the platform is in use.

PUT /api/v1/ai/admin/platform/test-conne.description: Tests a provider configuration before creating an AI platform from it.

GET /api/v1/ai/admin/platform/{id}/test-.description: Tests whether the stored AI platform configuration can connect to its external provider.

PUT /api/v1/ai/admin/platform/{id}/test-.description: Tests a modified configuration for an existing platform without persisting the update.

GET /api/v1/ai/admin/deployment.description: Returns all configured AI deployments, including display name, provider platform, token-cost configuration, aggregate cost, currency, and token limits.

POST /api/v1/ai/admin/deployment.description: Creates a new AI deployment and optionally validates provider connectivity before saving. Deployments link a model/deployment name to an AI platform together with token-cost settings.

parameters.testConnection: When true, test connectivity before saving the deployment. Defaults to false.

GET /api/v1/ai/admin/deployment/{id}.description: Returns a single AI deployment with cost statistics in the configured currency.

PUT /api/v1/ai/admin/deployment/{id}.description: Updates model name, display name, platform link, cost currency, token costs, and max input tokens. Can optionally test connectivity and recalculate existing cost statistics with the new settings.

parameters.testConnection: When true, test connectivity before saving. Defaults to false.
parameters.updateExistingCosts: When true, recalculate existing deployment and job cost statistics using the new cost settings. Defaults to false.

DELETE /api/v1/ai/admin/deployment/{id}.description: Deletes a non-system-managed deployment after verifying no AI endpoint references it.

PUT /api/v1/ai/admin/deployment/test-con.description: Tests an AI deployment configuration before it is saved.

GET /api/v1/ai/admin/deployment/{id}/tes.description: Sends a simple chat-completion test through the configured platform and deployment.

GET /api/v1/ai/admin/endpoint.description: Returns AI endpoints with linked deployment names, endpoint type, option schema/defaults, management flags, and audit metadata.

POST /api/v1/ai/admin/endpoint.description: Creates a non-system-managed AI endpoint linked to a deployment. Optionally sends a test chat completion before saving.

parameters.testConnection: When true, test the endpoint before saving. Defaults to false.

GET /api/v1/ai/admin/endpoint/{id}.description: Returns a single AI endpoint with the linked deployment display name and configuration metadata.

PUT /api/v1/ai/admin/endpoint/{id}.description: Updates a non-system-managed endpoint name, endpoint type, default options, deployment link, and options schema. Can optionally test connectivity before saving.

parameters.testConnection: When true, test the endpoint before saving. Defaults to false.

DELETE /api/v1/ai/admin/endpoint/{id}.description: Deletes a non-system-managed endpoint after verifying no AI job references it.

PUT /api/v1/ai/admin/endpoint/test-conne.description: Tests endpoint settings before creating or updating an endpoint.

GET /api/v1/ai/admin/endpoint/{id}/test-.description: Sends a simple chat-completion test through the stored endpoint configuration.

GET /api/v1/ai/endpoint.description: Returns the AI endpoints available to the caller (just id and name), ordered by name. Used by client code to enumerate endpoints before calling chat or completion.

POST /api/v1/ai/endpoint/{id}/chat.summary: Get chat completion via AI endpoint
POST /api/v1/ai/endpoint/{id}/chat.description: Sends a chat-completion request through the supplied AI endpoint with options, message list, and optional function definitions. Returns the model response under `response`.

POST /api/v1/ai/endpoint/{id}/completion.description: Sends a text-completion request through the supplied AI endpoint with options and a prompt. Returns the model response under `response`.

GET /api/v1/ai/platform-definition.description: Returns the known AI platform definitions (provider templates) with their id, name, configuration JSON schema, `SystemManaged` flag, and whether they support test-connection.

GET /api/v1/ai/platform-definition/{id}/.description: Returns the embedded logo image for a platform definition. Public — allowed anonymously. Response is cached for 24 hours.

GET /api/v1/aijob.description: Returns AI Agent jobs, including enabled state, scheduled execution metadata, endpoint cost rollups, metadata, and count of pending non-dry-run results.

parameters.metadataFilter: Optional key/value metadata filters as repeated `metadataFilter[key]=value` parameters.

POST /api/v1/aijob.description: Creates an AI Agent job with prompt, allowed skills, endpoint, schedule settings, data sources, metadata, and enabled state. Enabled scheduled jobs are scheduled immediately.

GET /api/v1/aijob/summary.description: Returns cross-job summary information such as approval rate for non-dry-run AI job results.

GET /api/v1/aijob/{id}.description: Returns a single AI Agent job with prompt, data sources, endpoint/deployment cost settings, result summary, provider definition, and metadata.

PUT /api/v1/aijob/{id}.description: Replaces an AI job configuration (prompt, metadata, allowed skills, schedule, endpoint, enabled state, data-source configuration). Scheduling is updated to match the saved state.

PATCH /api/v1/aijob/{id}.description: Applies a JSON Patch document to an AI job update model, then saves the resulting job configuration. Useful when only a few fields need to change.

DELETE /api/v1/aijob/{id}.description: Deletes an AI job only when it has no approved results. Jobs with approved results are retained for auditability.

POST /api/v1/aijob/{id}/execution-queue.description: Starts a non-dry-run distributed execution for an enabled and valid AI job. Returns the started distributed job id.

POST /api/v1/aijob/{id}/dry-run.description: Runs an AI job as a dry run using draft update settings without committing those settings as the live job configuration.

POST /api/v1/aijob/{id}/preview.description: Builds a preview of the prompt input for a job using current or draft settings. Useful for validating that the data source returns rows and that the prompt context is shaped correctly.

GET /api/v1/aijob/{id}/chatstream.description: Returns stream items from the latest job run chat stream, optionally after an offset.

GET /api/v1/aijob/{id}/runs.description: Returns paged job runs, filtered by run type (normal, test/dry, or all).

parameters.runType: Run type filter, for example `TestRun` or `Normal`.

GET /api/v1/aijob/{id}/runs/{runId}/resu.description: Returns paged results generated by a specific job run, with access-control filtering applied by skill.

GET /api/v1/aijob/{jobId}/runs/{runId}/c.description: Returns paged chat records for a specific AI job run, optionally restricted to chats that produced no results.

parameters.includeOnlyChatsWithNoResults: When true, return only chat rows with zero results.

GET /api/v1/aijob/{jobId}/runs/{runId}/c.description: Returns one chat record from a job run.

GET /api/v1/aijob/{id}/results/{type}.summary: List AI job results by type
GET /api/v1/aijob/{id}/results/{type}.description: Returns paged non-dry-run job results of a given result type, filtered by state and search text, with access-control filtering applied by skill.

parameters.searchName: Optional text filter over result description.

GET /api/v1/aijob/{id}/results/{type}/{r.description: Returns one non-dry-run result for the job and result type.

POST /api/v1/aijob/{id}/results/{type}/a.summary: Approve or reject selected job results
POST /api/v1/aijob/{id}/results/{type}/a.description: Applies an approval action to selected results of the given result type.

PUT /api/v1/aijob/{id}/bulk-result-appro.summary: Bulk approve or reject job results
PUT /api/v1/aijob/{id}/bulk-result-appro.description: Applies an approval action to all non-dry-run results for a job and returns counts for successful, skipped, and errored items.

GET /api/v1/aijobskills.description: Returns AI Agent skills available to the organisation, including skill id, display description, auto-approval state, and supported primary data-source types.

GET /api/copilot/getAllChats.description: Returns the calling user's Copilot chat sessions (sorted newest first), each populated with messages.

GET /api/copilot/getChat/{chatId}.description: Returns a single Copilot chat session by id along with its messages.

POST /api/copilot/createChatSession.description: Creates a Copilot chat session with the supplied title and language for the calling user.

PUT /api/copilot/updateChatSession.description: Updates an existing Copilot chat session (typically its title).

POST /api/copilot/chat.description: Adds a user message to a Copilot chat session. The chat continuation runs through SignalR (`CopilotHub`); the HTTP response is 202 Accepted as soon as the message is queued.

POST /api/copilot/suggestions.description: Returns suggested prompts for the current URL across the user's available Copilot skills, translated into the requested language when needed.

GET /api/copilot/chat/{chatId}/examples.description: Returns grouped example prompts for the chat's language (falling back to English).

POST /api/copilot/reprocess/{chatId}.description: Reprocesses the last failed turn in a Copilot chat session. Returns 404 when the chat cannot be found.

POST /api/powerfx/lsp.description: Channels Language Server Protocol messages between the Power Fx formula bar in the UI and the in-process LanguageServer, used to power intellisense, hover info, and diagnostics for rule expressions. Returns 400 with the error message when the LSP processing fails.

GET /api/powerfx/context.description: Returns a sample JSON context document showing the shape of the `Entity` record (properties, tags, aliases, identifiers, outgoing edges) available to Power Fx expressions in the given scope.
